title: Dark Matter Direct Detection Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Dark Matter Direct Detection Inversion: Dark matter direct detection: set limits on WIMP-nucleus cross section from observed recoil spectrum. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.recoil.wimp_nucleus…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=10 with effective condition number kappa_eff~1000; halo_model_uncertainty, nuclear_form_factor_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dR/dE_R = N_T * rho_DM / (m_DM * m_T) * integral_{v_min}^{inf} v*f(v) * d_sigma/d_E_R dv
E.world_state_x: WIMP mass m_DM and spin-independent cross section sigma_SI
E.observation_y: observed recoil event spectrum N_obs(E_R) in energy bins above threshold

W.regime: Existence of the recovered WIMP_cross_section_mass is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 1000); halo_model_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Poisson sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: sigma_SI limit scales as 1/exposure; background limited at low mass. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~1000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |halo_model_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_bins * N_mass_points * N_sigma_points) for exclusion plane per iteration
